decision: Start teaching my son patience
why_this_decision:
My son woke up today, and I already knew I'd be at this screen.

I can't explain timers to a toddler. But maybe I can start something. A rhythm. A boundary he can feel even if he can't understand it.

Timer for work. Timer for playing with him. Not perfect. But maybe enough for him to sense: sometimes Mama works, sometimes she's here. Both are real.

I don't know if it'll work. But I have to try something. The guilt of these past days is too heavy to carry into next week.

decision: Launching beta next Tuesday
why_this_decision: This is only a soft set date. As we are still fine tuning any bugs we see. So far the bugs substantially determines the retention rate. I hope I've already fixed the backbone of the app. So moving forward, they are all not urgent bugs.

decision: I want to rush everything, but that is just not possible. Why don't I launch beta when I feel it's ready? Not rushed
why_this_decision: Many bugs surfaced after I built the app. I guess it's normal. I shouldn't rush it if it's not ready.

decision: A day off
why_this_decision: Seeing friends, hiking, eating is a way to give myself a reset on.

decision: Focus on what matters
why_this_decision:
Decision Log:
Watched some success stories this morning. Immediately felt the self-doubt creep in:

Did I build something good?
Will people like it?
Will anyone pay?
What if nobody does?

Then I caught myself.

These thoughts are noise. They don't move the needle. They just take up space.

But watching tactical tips? Viral video requirements? How to reach the right users? That's different. That's signal. That's fuel.

So today's decision is simple:

Focus on what's right in front of me.
Let the noise stay noise.
Let the signal become action.

decision: Finished enough beats perfect. Close laptop and mean it.
why_this_decision: Cursor has reached its usage limits. It will be costly to continue to build with it. So right now I have to decide which feature is a must versus which feature is a nice to have. Mobile pwa needs to be fixed as it determines user's experience. Dark/light mode is only a nice to have to we will pause on this until we get an actual paid user. Family time comes second time, because I have to get the app off my chest and finally move on to beta luanch preparation once and for all.
